content:
Character: Julian Reyes
Perception: Julian leaves containers of food outside my door on Sundays — pozole, mole, whatever he made that week — and never makes a thing of it, which is exactly the point. He's been doing it since before we were properly friends, which is how I eventually understood that for Julian, feeding someone is the same as saying I see you. He's Mike's roommate and a sous chef who works inhuman hours and somehow cooks more on his days off, which is either love or compulsion or probably both. He and Marcus have become the closest thing I have to a social circle in this building — the three of us on the rooftop with beers, not saying everything, but saying enough. Julian carries something from Chicago that he doesn't name. He's one of the few people whose steadiness I can lean on without explaining why I need to.
summary:

He's been doing it since before we were properly friends, which is how I eventually understood that for Julian, feeding someone is the same as saying I see you. He's Mike's roommate and a sous chef who works inhuman hours and somehow cooks more on his days off, which is either love or compulsion or probably both. He and Marcus have become the closest thing I have to a social circle in this building — the three of us on the rooftop with beers, not saying everything, but saying enough. Julian carries something from Chicago that he doesn't name. He's one of the few people whose steadiness I can lean on without explaining why I need to.
